name: La Tabarla — Circular from the Forata Reservoir
name_original: La Tabarla — Circular desde el Embalse de Forata
region_intro: Mountain biking around Embalse de Forata offers diverse terrain surrounding the reservoir, which is fed by the Rio Magro. The region features a mix of paved, unpaved, and gravel surfaces, providing varied riding experiences. Trails often incorporate elements like berms and rock gardens, with routes catering to different skill levels from moderate to difficult. The landscape includes serene water views, river valleys, and natural features, making it suitable for exploring by mountain bike. Best mountain bike trails around Embalse de Forata The most popular mountain bike trail is La Tabarla — Circular from the Forata Reservoir, a 35.4 miles (56.9 km) trail that takes 5 hours 12 minutes to complete. This difficult route requires advanced riding skills and good fitness. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Forata Dam – Forata Reservoir loop from Yátova, a moderate 24.1 miles (38.8 km) path. This route offers scenic views around the reservoir and is suitable for riders with good fitness. Local mountain bikers also love the Fuente la Canaleja – Rio Magro (Hortunas de Abajo) loop from Tabarla, a 42.1 miles (67.7 km) trail leading through river valleys and varied terrain, often completed in about 5 hours 58 minutes. Mountain biking around Embalse de Forata is defined by reservoir views, diverse terrain with varying surfaces, and river landscapes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Embalse de Forata are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.2 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 200 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Embalse de Forata's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What are the difficulty levels of mountain bike trails around Embalse de Forata?
A: The mountain bike trails around Embalse de Forata cater to various skill levels. You'll find a good mix of moderate and difficult routes. There are 11 moderate trails and 7 difficult trails available, offering options for riders with good fitness to those requiring advanced riding skills.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the mountain bike trails in Embalse de Forata?
A: The terrain around Embalse de Forata is diverse, featuring a mix of paved, unpaved, and gravel surfaces. Trails often incorporate dynamic elements like berms and rock gardens, providing a varied riding experience. You'll encounter river valleys and scenic views around the reservoir.

Q: Are there any notable viewpoints or natural features to see along the trails?
A: Yes, the area is known for its picturesque environment. Many routes offer serene water views of the Embalse de Forata itself, fed by the Rio Magro. You might also catch scenic vistas of other nearby natural features like the Embalse de la Muela and Cortes de Pallás, allowing for close contact with nature.

Q: When is the best time of year to go mountain biking around Embalse de Forata?
A: The region's climate generally allows for outdoor activities throughout much of the year. However, for the most comfortable riding conditions, spring and autumn are often ideal, avoiding the peak heat of summer and potential colder, wetter conditions in winter. Always check local weather forecasts before heading out.

Q: Is there parking available for mountain bikers near the trails?
A: While specific parking locations aren't detailed, many routes start from towns like Yátova or Alborache, or other accessible points around the reservoir, implying parking is generally available in these areas. It's advisable to check the starting point of your chosen route for specific parking information.

Q: Can I reach the mountain bike trails by public transport?
A: The region research mentions towns like Yátova and Alborache as starting points for some routes. While direct public transport to every trailhead might be limited, these towns may offer connections. For specific public transport options, it's best to research local bus services to Yátova or Alborache and plan your route from there.

Q: Do I need any special permits to mountain bike around Embalse de Forata?
A: The provided information does not indicate that special permits are required for mountain biking in the Embalse de Forata area. However, it's always good practice to respect local regulations and private property signs.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes available?
A: Yes, many routes around Embalse de Forata are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31848105" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Forata Dam – Forata Reservoir loop from Yátova&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate option, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31505678" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente la Canaleja – Rio Magro (Hortunas de Abajo) loop from Tabarla&lt;/a&gt; offers a longer, more challenging circular ride.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about mountain biking in Embalse de Forata?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.2 stars from over 10 reviews. Reviewers often praise the diverse terrain, the beautiful and picturesque environment, and the serene water views of the reservoir, making it ideal for combining sport with nature appreciation.

Q: How many mountain bike trails are there in total around Embalse de Forata?
A: There are 18 mountain bike tours available around Embalse de Forata on komoot, offering a wide range of options for exploration.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. For experienced riders seeking a challenge, routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e910825824/la-tabarla-circular-from-the-forata-reservoir" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Tabarla — Circular from the Forata Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, a 56.9 km trail, require advanced riding skills and very good fitness. Another difficult option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31488909" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Casa del Coronel (Mijares) – Mijares River loop from Casas de la Paridera&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 41 km.

Q: Are the mountain bike trails suitable for families or beginners?
A: While there are no trails specifically categorized as 'easy' in the guide data, there are 11 moderate routes that could be suitable for families with some riding experience or beginners looking for a bit of a challenge. These routes generally require good fitness but are less technically demanding than the difficult trails.
tips: Small and picturesque house in a very quiet environment where you can rest and relax to continue the ascent towards the Pico del Ñoño
It's a beautiful spot where you cross the Magro River in a very picturesque setting. You can reach it via the CV-429 road or the GR-238 footpath.

From the road you cross the bridge over the Mijares River, a very colorful and picturesque point. In summer it can be a good place to take a refreshing swim. From here you can connect with the GR-7 paths or the paved secondary road that reaches the town of Mijares.
Road for mtb in a very picturesque environment with very nice views of the Picando Ravine, the Picastre Stream until reaching the Forata Reservoir and the dam of the same name.

The Forata reservoir is located in the municipality of Yátova, in the province of Valencia. It is located on the bed of the Rio Magro and its construction dates back to 1969.
The environment is very beautiful and picturesque with several hiking and mtb routes to cover it practically all the way around.
